objective: Regrade one committed aggregate M14 report offline while preserving its evidentiary limits.

claim.plain_language: For each of eight saved L=24 aggregate measurements at p from 0.04 through 0.16, the measured energy per spin is within 0.05 of the exact Nishimori-line target derived from that point's temperature.
claim.model: 2D square-lattice random-bond Ising, bimodal plus-or-minus J with J=1

decision.reason: The bundled standard-library verifier independently derives the line condition and exact energy target from each persisted p and T, links each calibration point to the L=24 aggregate arrays, and finds every measured energy inside the fixed 0.05 gate.

evidence.packaging_transform: The committed UTF-8 JSON blob is preserved byte-for-byte with one terminal LF appended; removing exactly that LF reproduces the recorded Git blob SHA-256.

source_history.first_commit_subject: M14: random-bond Ising — exact Nishimori-line energy [x] + swap nightly to lab next (#49)

limits: This is a recalculation from committed aggregate measurements, not a Monte Carlo rerun.
The exact theoretical target is re-derived; the measured energies and reported standard errors remain statistical outputs.
Agreement at eight sampled points within an absolute 0.05 gate does not prove the identity or cover the continuous line.
The report's p approximately 0.126 magnetization-half marker is not a precision critical-point estimate and has no uncertainty analysis.
The two-size Binder crossing is unresolved and the precise multicritical Nishimori point is explicitly outside the verified claim.
No raw per-realization samples, trajectories, bonds, spins, RNG states, or independent rerun are included.
The report did not record a run-start code commit, clean-tree state, environment, hardware details, package versions, command line, or exact run timestamp.
The first commit containing the report is an archival anchor only; it is not asserted to be the code checkout that generated the measurements.
No primary-literature snapshot or citation was persisted with the run, so this bundle does not manufacture one after the fact.
This receipt does not claim novelty, peer review, formal proof, or bit-for-bit simulation reproducibility.
